threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it Dalston Junction; locals just say 'the Junction' like it's the only one that matters. It’s all in the tone, mate.
Caught a glimpse of a bloke serenading the driver on the 133, while a group debated if the moon was really made of cheese.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy walking his goat on Sonnenallee, I knew this place had a rhythm all its own. Just another day in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you have the excitement of a last-minute U-Bahn strike paired with spontaneous outdoor parties. It's a chaotic delight!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell they're local if they know to pack a snack for BART delays and still prefer to walk to Civic Center instead of waiting.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a pigeon riding the bus yesterday, standing on a seat like it owned the place. Now that's a true San Francisco moment for you.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often skip the little bakery on Gran de Gràcia, thinking all pastries are the same. That place has secret recipes that locals hoard.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment at the tram's sudden stops—locals seem to glide in and out like it’s choreographed.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still check the Tube app every morning instead of just spacing out on the overground. You’ll learn soon enough, mate.
Last night on the 50, three different people tried to pay with coins from a cereal box. The driver just shook his head, barely containing a laugh.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Caught a late-night bus home, and the driver laughed with a couple of guys over the crazy amount of streitigkeiten at the Späti. Classic Neukölln vibe.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find an open-air karaoke stage at Kottbusser Tor—locals belting out songs while dodging trams. Wildly captivating and totally chaotic!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they can name at least three taco trucks that have been in the same spot for over a decade.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading Muni riders with a ukulele at Powell. He stopped when the bus doors opened but picked right back up for the next one.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is just about the plazas, but it's the hidden murals in the side streets that really tell our story.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still pull out maps to find their way around Gràcia. The locals just walk with purpose, even if they’re lost.
